mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments that require efficient orchestration of large language models, offering features like intelligent inference scheduling and workload autoscaling. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware accelerators and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the system path.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster, allowing kubectl to interact with it.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command sets up monitoring for your deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment llm-d --all

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes all llm-d deployments.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/upgrade.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Apply the upgrade configuration.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and ensure all configurations are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not have the correct permissions to access the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your user has the necessary roles assigned in GCP IAM.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, ensure to update your scheduling configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in 'Running' status and 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
